persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: single initial 'C' with 17 Hartley officials and 13 Hartley biographies - a very common surname, the textbook namesake trap. Why it fails: the position string 'Lecturer in English and Modern Languages, Royal College' matches near-verbatim (sim 97) and is a single, highly specific post; the bio's lecturer->principal arc at the same Royal College is reproduced in the stint (lecturer 1899-1900, principal from 1905), and 11 edition-years co-occur with the colony-agreeing position. Among many Hartleys only one held this exact Royal College lectureship and then its principalship.

evidence_cited.fact: lecturer in English and modern languages, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 11th Dec., 1896
Lecturer in English and Modern Languages, Royal College

persons.would_overturn: Another C. Hartley biography also recorded as lecturer in English/modern languages at the Royal College in the same span.

notes: Common surname and single initial, but the verbatim specific position match plus 11 edition cooccurrences clear the bar.
